result:
All 6 tests pass. There were two bugs in `lrucache/cache.py`:

1. **`get` didn't refresh recency** — a cache hit returned the value without moving the entry to the most-recently-used position, so a just-read key could still be evicted first. Fixed by adding `self._data.move_to_end(key)` on a successful, non-expired hit (`cache.py:30`).

2. **`put` evicted on in-place updates** — the eviction check ran even when the key already existed, so overwriting a key in a full cache wrongly evicted another entry despite the size not growing. Fixed by only evicting when `key not in self._data` (`cache.py:34`).

`SOLUTION.md` is written with the same explanation, and `python3 -m pytest -q` reports `6 passed`.
